algorithms.title: Interval Scheduling & Merging
algorithms.description: Interval problems involve finding overlapping intervals, merging them, or scheduling the maximum number of non-overlapping events. Core patterns: sort by start or end time, then sweep. Merge Overlapping Intervals: O(N log N). Activity Selection (maximum non-overlapping): sort by end time, greedily pick earliest-ending. Meeting Rooms II (minimum rooms): sort + min-heap or events sweep.
algorithms.intuition: Sorting is always the first step for interval problems. Key insight for Activity Selection: greedily pick the activity that ENDS EARLIEST. This leaves the maximum remaining time for future activities. For merging: sort by start time; if current interval's start <= previous end, they overlap — extend the end. For minimum rooms: count maximum concurrent events — treat start/end times as separate events, sweep and track active count.
algorithms.steps: [{"step":1,"description":"Merge Overlapping: sort by start. For each interval: if it overlaps with last merged (start <= last.end), extend end. Else add new interval."},{"step":2,"description":"Activity Selection: sort by end time. Greedily pick interval if start >= last picked end."},{"step":3,"description":"Meeting Rooms II: use min-heap of end times. Sort by start. For each meeting: if heap.peek() <= start, reuse room (poll). Push end time. Answer = heap size."}]
algorithms.spaceComplexity: O(N) for merged intervals or heap
